title: Post 3rd Impact EoE image from the DS official artbook
display_title: Post 3rd Impact EoE image from the DS official artbook
wikitext:
[]: Post 3rd Impact EoE image from the DS official artbook.             Quote Post by Mario_Abel » Sun Nov 16, 2014 9:25 pm I'm not sure if this is chit-chat or not, but I ended up uploading this to reddit just recently and I'm surprised many not have had the chance to glance at this picture..as it is stunning to look at indeed. After seeing EoE a few times, the Final ending sequence was loading my mind with questions on what would become of Shinji and Asuka's future in reality. I was looking at some scans from the Die Sterne artbook a few days ago and...... behold! What are Evageeks thoughts on this? Does this artwork change your perspective on things regarding the finale? For me, this signifies an ending of true love and hope for the teenagers, despite 3rd impact having resolved on earth. Post your thoughts!

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Nov 16, 2014 10:05 pm I've seen this pic plenty of times before -- but forgot (if I ever knew) that it was official artwork! As much as i know there's a lot of official artwork we really shouldn't take too seriously, fuck it, I want to believe !

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sun Nov 16, 2014 10:39 pm I saw this picture many years ago on this forum, but I think it was stated it's from a doujinshi. Though I can't for the life of me remember the name of the artist.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Nov 16, 2014 11:09 pm Just because it's official artwork doesn't mean that it ever happened... but it's nice to think it did.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Nov 16, 2014 11:16 pm ^ Plus, EoE was open-ended for a reason, so the plausabilities could be INFINITE. Though, my bag sticks with this pice.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Nov 16, 2014 11:41 pm It is official, drawn by Takeshi Okazaki for the 2001 Movic calendar. This is an incredibly useful link: http://evacommentary.org/omake/die-sterne/die-sterne.html

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Nov 17, 2014 4:28 am so, no underpants, huh? What's up with that? Also, Shinji looks like he's 11 in the artwork

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Nov 17, 2014 4:32 am So all that LCL just cleaned itself up? sure why not. Somebody should get on removing those MP EVA's though, don't want what is essentially a giant penis as an eyesore in the destroyed city.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Mon Nov 17, 2014 12:31 pm xanderkh wrote: ^ Plus, EoE was open-ended for a reason, so the plausabilities could be INFINITE. Though, my bag sticks with this pice.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 17, 2014 12:45 pm This is the background on my iPhone. I can't quite buy into it (blue sea, and they look way too . . . calm), but it's my favorite piece of Eva artwork nonetheless.

[]:             Quote Post by Coldwire » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:01 pm Well, I saw it before, here, I think, while it is hard to imagine things would work out like that, I want to believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:02 pm Bagheera wrote: This is the background on my iPhone. I can't quite buy into it (blue sea, and they look way too . . . calm), but it's my favorite piece of Eva artwork nonetheless.

[]:             Quote Post by Mario_Abel » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:03 pm I bought a Die Sterne 2.0 artbook last saturday from Amazon at a decent price tag. I hope I don't receive a bootleg copy though (wouldn't think they'd be able to sell pirated copies of this book anyway).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:13 pm Jäeger wrote: IMO the image seems to take place a lot of time after the beach(when I'm talking about, so I've been never been troubled with the blue water or the way they act. Who Knows what happened in Japan??

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Mon Nov 17, 2014 1:26 pm Bagheera wrote: Yeah, I've tried to talk myself into that take, but the hitch is that Asuka probably would have removed her bandages by then. No reason to keep them on forever.

[]:             Quote Post by The Fireballed Mage » Mon Nov 17, 2014 4:35 pm Easily my favorite picture in all of Eva-dom. (It's hanging on my wall at the moment.) After all the hurt, missed chances, losses, and sometimes down right misery of Eva, it's a simple picture of hope. "Will you take my hand?" "Yes, I will."

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Nov 18, 2014 12:53 am There doesn't need to be rhyme or reason for the setting in that photo. It's just nice and calm and peaceful.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Nov 19, 2014 12:29 pm ^ A perfect way to balance out Eva, to show that what Shinji and Asuka fought for was actually worth something to gain in the end. Peace and calmness.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Dec 03, 2014 9:07 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: This is an incredibly useful link: http://evacommentary.org/omake/die-sterne/die-sterne.html

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Dec 03, 2014 10:24 pm I wouldn't put too much stock in it. "Official" art for shows like this depict a lot of shit. Although yes it would be nice.
